calendarFaqs.q: When does school start for Sacramento City Unified School District in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: The first day of school for Sacramento City Unified School District is Tuesday, August 11, 2026. Students and staff are expected to report on this date to begin the new academic year.

calendarFaqs.q: When is the last day of school for Sacramento City Unified School District in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: The last day of school for Sacramento City Unified School District is Thursday, May 27, 2027. The school year runs from August through May, covering 180 instructional days.

calendarFaqs.q: When is spring break 2026-2027 for Sacramento City Unified School District?
calendarFaqs.a: Spring break for Sacramento City Unified School District runs Mar 23, 2027–Mar 27, 2027, 5 days.

calendarFaqs.q: When is winter break 2026-2027 for Sacramento City Unified School District?
calendarFaqs.a: Winter break for Sacramento City Unified School District runs Dec 21, 2026–Jan 4, 2027, 15 days. School resumes Jan 5, 2027.

yearNumbers.label: Fewer Instructional Days
yearNumbers.detail: SCUSD drops from 188 instructional days in 2025-2026 to the standard 180 — a reduction of 8 days, with the year ending 15 days earlier (May 27 vs June 11).

yearNumbers.label: Starts Earlier
yearNumbers.detail: First day is August 11 vs August 18 in 2025-2026 — a full week earlier, which will affect summer program and childcare planning.

yearNumbers.label: Indigenous Peoples Day
yearNumbers.detail: Sacramento City Unified observes Indigenous Peoples Day as a school holiday — not a universal California school holiday, and often missed by families new to the district.

yearNumbers.label: César Chávez Day
yearNumbers.detail: César Chávez Day on March 31 falls four days after Spring Break ends, creating a second no-school day in the same week that students return from break.

calendarNotes: Classes begin August 11, 2026, and end May 27, 2027. Winter Break runs December 21–January 4; Spring Break follows on March 23–27. Because César Chávez Day falls on March 31 — four days after Spring Break ends — late March includes two separate no-school periods within the same two-week window. February also includes Lincoln's Birthday on the 8th and Presidents' Day on the 15th, placing two California state holidays one week apart.
